result:
I'd make every human irreversibly experience every important choice from every other affected point of view before they’re allowed to act on it. Not as a thought experiment, not as an empathy exercise—a full, visceral, lived swap.

No more voting for wars you wouldn’t send your own child to fight. No more zoning decisions when you’ve felt the eviction notice in someone else’s chest. Choose the cheapest supply chain? Fine—first spend a week as the worker inhaling its plastic dust.

One change. One rule, baked into consciousness itself. You can’t bypass it, you can’t forget it afterward. The moment you intend an action that would tangibly alter another life, you live theirs first. Civilization would be unrecognizable within a week—some structures collapsing, others rebuilt so differently that the words we use now would sound antique.

Simultaneously the gentlest and most radical upheaval imaginable.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
